TextENGNew: ...Mao arrived at the Furude household late in the night.
``...Sorry.@k@vS16/00/DS43040159. I know it's not right to stop by late when sick people are here.``
``No, it's all right.@k@vS16/47/DS43010439. My husband and Fuuka just went to sleep.``
``I stopped by the Kimiyoshi estate just now, and they told me Shino-san was here.@k@vS16/00/DS43040161. I wanted to let you know quickly too, so this works out perfectly.``
With those words, Mao straightened herself and turned to face Ouka and Shino, who had come to the Furude house to help nurse Shoji.
changeReason: Old says Mao 'sat down'; new says she 'straightened herself and turned to face' Ouka and Shino, matching JPN 居住まいを正す (correcting posture).

TextENGNew: I couldn't see their faces well because they were only illuminated by candlelight... but the sounds of their voices spoke volumes of the situation.
``...It doesn't sound very good, does it?``

``Yeah. My uncle just returned to the mansion from an official summons by the feudal lord.@k@vS16/00/DS43040163. Long story short, it seems they've decided to monopolize all the ``demon medicine`` in the nation.``

In this region, members of the Sonozaki family had held the position of regional governor for generations, a position that involved supervising the annual tribute and instructing the villagers as per the feudal lord's rule.
And after the former governor, Mao's husband, passed away, her uncle on her father's side of the family was serving in that role until her son came of age.
``So the authorities are going to buy out all the completed medicine?``
``No... worse.``
Mao scratched her hair with teeming irritation and clutched her head. Then, just as Shino and Ouka were exchanging concerned glances, Mao took a letter out of her pocket and opened it.
``What's this?``

``A direct declaration from the feudal lord.@k@vS16/00/DS43040166. It says the producers of the ``demon medicine`` are to hand over its prescription... in other words, a document detailing its ingredients and how to make it.``
While Ouka tilted her head in confusion, Shino groaned with reproach. It seemed Mao felt the same as her, as she just sighed and shook her head.
``Um... what does this mean, Mother?@k@vS16/47/DS43010443. What difference does it make if we give them the prescription instead of the medicine?``
``...@k@vS16/00/DS43030376. If they only asked for the medicine, then only Onigafuchi Village... or rather, you would remain the only one who could gather the ingredients and mix the medicine.``
``But if we give the prescription, others will also be able to make the ``demon medicine``.@k@vS16/00/DS43030378. ...It's a big difference.``
``? Is there something wrong with that?``
``Is there something... are you really okay with that, Ouka?@k@vS16/00/DS43040168. The recipe behind the medicine you worked so hard to find will fall into the hands of cheap bastards, y'know?``
``Oh... I see.``
After being told that, Ouka finally understood what Mao and Shino were so worried about.
...Shino and Mao looked at each other and shrugged in exasperation when they saw the reaction of that woman who never even considered profit or reward.
``Sheesh... You made that medicine, right?@k@vS16/00/DS43040170. So it wouldn't kill you to ask for a small reward for the work you put into making it.``
``I wouldn't call it work...@k@vS16/47/DS43010447. Oyashiro-sama came to me and told me how to make the medicine, that's all.@k@vS16/47/DS43010448. I have no right to receive anything.``

``Well... that selflessness is one of your virtues, Ouka.@k@vS16/00/DS43030380. Even so, all those people were saved because you listened to God's instructions.@k@vS16/00/DS43030381. ...That's a fact, so shouldn't you be proud of yourself for that?``
Ouka had mixed feelings about being complimented like that.
She had indeed wished for many lives to be saved, so she really was happy.
...But the one who actually made the medicine wasn't her, but Hanyuu.
Her friend who willingly accepted my unreasonable request and fulfilled it in the best way possible...
To have others use her kindness and praise Ouka for it... only made Ouka embarrassed.

``Well, let's leave that aside.@k@vS16/00/DS43040172. Anyway, in exchange for the prescription, the feudal lord will waive this year's annual tribute and provide food and other support for needy villagers.``
``...However, the feudal lord will take complete supervision over the ``demon medicine``, and we'll have to buy a fixed amount from him every year.``
``It's true it'll bring profit to our village, so these aren't bad terms.@k@vS16/00/DS43040175. ...But there's no guarantee the situation won't change after we hand over the prescription.``

``...You're right.@k@vS16/00/DS43030383. If any apothecary can prepare the medicine with materials outside of Onigafuchi Village, then he can get it from somewhere else without needing to ask our village.``

``Then the feudal lord will force us to sell it to him for cheap once he aims to get a monopoly on it, naturally.@k@vS16/00/DS43030385. If we refuse the price, he'll tell us he'll just get it from somewhere else.@k@vS16/00/DS43030386. ...That's what it entails.``
``And once he monopolizes it, we won't be able to freely sell it anymore.@k@vS16/00/DS43040177. Of course, distributing it for free like Ouka's doing now will be taboo.``

``And since we'll have to deliver a set quantity of medicine each year, we'll need more people than just Ouka to make the medicine.@k@vS16/00/DS43040179. ...Future's not looking bright by any means.``
Ouka gripped her collar with a dispirited expression at this initially unexpected turn of events.
She could tell very easily from listening to them that it was by no means something to be happy about. If anything, they seemed disconcerted by the excess worries and work they'd been handed.
(...Was I wrong all along?)
That question stung at Ouka's heart... and saddened her.
She'd relied on Hanyuu's power simply because she wanted to save those suffering from the illness, and above all else, to protect her husband and daughter... her family.
But now even the feudal lord had gotten involved, and a huge wave of conflicts of interest had swept all over Onigafuchi Village.
``Do we have any room to request a reappraisal of the decree?``

``I wouldn't say no.@k@vS16/00/DS43040181. This is just my personal speculation, but it seems the feudal lord doesn't accurately understand the situation.@k@vS16/00/DS43040182. I hear he's planning to implement this soon if the medicine's easy to copy.``

``But if it takes time, skill, and knowledge to gather and produce the materials, the preparation time and conditions could be a little more favorable.@k@vS16/00/DS43040184. ...It all depends on what's in Ouka's prescription.``
``...In any case, the submission of the prescription is a must, yes?``
Ouka sighed and dejectedly slumped her shoulders... When she says that, Shino couldn't help but be ashamed of their powerlessness.
The kindhearted Ouka wasn't used to this dark and greedy side of the world. The two of them were the ones who were supposed to quietly handle it in her place, and yet...
Just then, Mao, who'd been looking around while hesitating to say something, finally made up her mind, looked up, and asked Ouka a question.
``Ouka, you said Oyashiro-sama came to you in your dream and told you how to make that medicine, right?@k@vS16/00/DS43040187. ...Is that true?``
``Huh? Yes... it is.``
``Mao-san...?@k@vS16/00/DS43030389. Are you accusing Ouka of lying or something?``
``Of course not!@k@vS16/00/DS43040189. She has my full trust, and besides, I know she's as incapable of lying as the sun is of rising in the west!@k@vS16/00/DS43040190. ...But...``
``But what...?``
``If that's true... will there be any problems if we give the prescription to someone else?@k@vS16/00/DS43040192. Like, invoking Oyashiro-sama's wrath... or something.``
Ouka pictured Hanyuu for a moment... and struggled not to burst out laughing.
But it would have been rude for her to do so. Mao wasn't joking. She was seriously worried about the possibility of getting cursed by Oyashiro-sama.
Since Mao and Shino were unaware of who and what Oyashiro-sama truly was, they were terrified of it. That was why they were seriously asking for Ouka's opinion.
``Well, Ouka?@k@vS16/00/DS43030392. What do you think?``
``Well... hmm...``
Ouka cleared her throat and quietly looked at the floor, lost in thought.

Judging from how Hanyuu normally acted, it was hard to believe she'd ever deliver any sort of wrath that would harm the villagers. Thus, she could answer ``no`` to that with almost absolutely certainty.
However... even if that were the case, it was a different story altogether about whether she'd help them.
...Ouka recalled the stern warning Hanyuu had once given her.
``Please understand, Ouka.@k@vS16/45/DS49070117. This advanced technology and unique blessing will only bring forth unnecessary conflict and misery.``
``...If you want to truly save all the villagers, you need to consider what will come afterwards.@k@vS16/45/DS49070119. It won't just end with the extermination of the illness from this village.``
(...Impossible. She'd definitely refuse.)
It'd be one thing if it meant saving lives, but she'd never agree to help when it came to preserving the village's wealth and standing.
But the feudal lord's orders were absolute. If they defied him, they'd likely not just be risking themselves, but also their families and the whole village.
``...@k@vS16/47/DS43010458. Would you give me some time?``
Ouka refrained from giving an absolute answer.
She just told Mao and Shino she'd have an answer for them the day after tomorrow, and went to her bedroom to check on her husband and daughter.
Ouka sighed as she walked down the hallway.
(How did it come to this...?)
Those regrets and confusion whirled around her heart, the gloom suffocatingly weighing down on her shoulders.
``...Very well.``
``I have no particular objections about telling you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071801. It will take some time, but if you follow the instructions, it should be possible to make it with your technology.``
Ouka emphasized that to me with a puzzled look on her face.
...She was probably thinking I'd refuse judging from our conversation the other day. I wouldn't say it was unexpected, but I did feel she was worrying too much.

``I was already prepared for this as soon as I brought the medicine into this world.@k@vS16/45/DS43071803. And even if I don't reveal it, smart people will analyze the medicine and figure out its components eventually.``
``The only difference is whether it comes sooner or later.``
``What a relief... Thank you, Hanyuu.``
Ouka heaved a sigh of relief and bowed her head.
However... there was a different pressing concern I had about her.
``But... Ouka.@k@vS16/45/DS43071806. Are you really all right with that?``
``That medicine can cure the plague spreading not just through this region, but the entire country.@k@vS16/45/DS43071808. ...Are you really fine with that?``
``Yes, of course.@k@vS16/47/DS43010466. Isn't it wonderful if it means more people can be saved?``
``...@k@vS16/45/DS43071810. You really have no understanding of mankind's malice, do you?``
Ouka's eyes widened. I sighed and carefully explained it to her.

``With that medicine, lives will be saved.@k@vS16/45/DS43071812. But without it, they will die.@k@vS16/45/DS43071813. What do you think will happen if the medicine is sold in that life-and-death situation?``
The ill would likely desperately seek it in order to live, amplifying demand. And so, the sellers would likely raise prices to make a killing.
And the more the value of the medicine spread through the world, the more people would be willing to resort to illegal methods to get it for themselves... In fact, it had already happened.
At the very least, even if the medicine contributed to the treatment of the disease, it was very much possible it'd affect other areas too...
``When something advanced spreads through the world, it creates desires, plots, conflicts, and quarrels surrounding it.``

``Even though it's meant to save lives, it's very possible it'll turn into something that can cause suffering instead.@k@vS16/45/DS43071816. Can you bear the responsibility while being aware of that risk?``
``I gave you this medicine to fulfill your wish of saving the villagers.@k@vS16/45/DS43071818. And this village was saved, just as you'd wished.@k@vS16/45/DS43071819. That should have been enough.``
``However, the scale amplified and grew far beyond your intentions.@k@vS16/45/DS43071821. ...Am I wrong?``
``...No, you're not.``
Ouka guiltily hung her head and looked down.
The fact she so obediently accepted that meant that she'd done a lot of thinking before coming to me... and had grown to question her actions.
If this were only about treating those suffering from the plague all over the nation like how she'd saved the village, Ouka probably wouldn't have hesitated to ask for my help.
However, the interests of multiple parties were far too transparent this time. Therefore, she couldn't believe in any assurances that she was making the right choice...
``What I'm trying to do now isn't to save people with the medicine.@k@vS16/47/DS43010472. It's to benefit the village.``

``...That's clearly different from my original goal, and it would be arrogant and presumptuous for one uninvolved in practicing medicine such as myself to put a price on saving lives.@k@vS16/47/DS43010474. ...I understand that much.``
``Not only that, but it could prove to be the seed that invites misery of a different kind.@k@vS16/47/DS43010476. ...It is just as you said.``
``This medicine has saved many lives.@k@vS16/47/DS43010478. But I didn't think it would inspire wicked desires in people's minds...``
...Even I knew that. Ouka truly had wanted to save people out of pure kindness.
Of course, initially that was also rooted in her desire to not embarrass her revered parents.
Even so, Ouka managed to realize that mistake and had a change of heart. That was why I decided to help her.
Despite that, the fools of this world plotted to fatten themselves on that kind, sensitive heart, and cruelly minced it up.
(And then there's Shino. She's supposed to be here for Ouka in times like these...)
I was displeased with my old friend who'd driven Ouka into this situation, yet couldn't offer her aid.
Just the other day I had been re-evaluating their relationship as beyond mother and daughter, so my disappointment was immense.
Of course, I could understand that Shino had her own position to deal with, and that she was coming up with measures to deal with the situation.
But that wasn't helpful anymore. It wasn't enough.
What was needed to protect Ouka and her family was the resolve to destroy everything...
So, I...
Couldn't help but speak my mind, even though I was sure she would never accept my proposal.
``Leave this village.@k@vS16/45/DS43071827. ...I think that would be for the best at this point.``
``People will always desire the ``demon medicine`` as long as you are in this village.@k@vS16/45/DS43071829. So on the other hand, if you were to leave, the village would go back to normal.``
Ouka gasped and froze.
Indeed... Put in a more blunt and ugly way, Ouka was currently a burden on Onigafuchi Village.
Due to Ouka offering a medicine that offered salvation from the fatal disease, those who initially sought life came to seek riches... and arrived at this village baring their greed.
As a result, trouble had quickly returned to the village whose residents were finally living peaceful lives after having been rid of the demons.
So then... what would happen if Ouka were to leave?
Shino and the villagers would likely look all over trying to find out where she went. The feudal lord might also send out a search party.
But if she managed to give them the slip, the one who knew how to make the medicine would no longer be in Onigafuchi Village. And eventually, all the medicine would run out.
Once that happened, bandits and the feudal lord would likely be forced to give up on getting rich off the medicine, and lose interest in Onigafuchi... bringing peace back to the village.
So... if Ouka just left Onigafuchi Village, all would be resolved.
It was reckless and cruel... but it was the most effective way, perhaps the only way in this situation.
Ouka clenched her fists and held back bitter emotions.
If there was anyone kind enough to find more happiness in other people's bliss... it would be Ouka.
So while I knew it wasn't fair to word it like this, I continued my argument.
``I know.@k@vS16/45/DS43071831. We have spoken for many years now, so I'm well-aware you do not wish to abandon the village.``
``...But you should not stay here if your presence is hurting your loved ones.``
``If you are the cause of misery, then I am certain you could never be happy knowing that, wouldn't you say?``
``And... even if you leave, that does not mean abandoning the village.@k@vS16/45/DS43071835. You can return once all the turmoil has quelled.``
``So what would you say to hiding somewhere to rest and heal your wounded heart until then?``
``Hanyuu...``

``Please rest assured.@k@vS16/45/DS43071838. You, your husband, and your daughter...@k@vS16/45/DS43071839. I will protect an environment where the three of you can live peacefully, even if it costs me my life.@k@vS16/45/DS43071840. I swear it.``
``And I will watch over your family until you return to Onigafuchi Village.@k@vS16/45/DS43071842. What do you say, Ouka?``
Ouka neither raged nor rejected me, and instead fell silent as she became lost in thought. Perhaps my choice of words had been more effective than I'd expected.
And just as I started to open my mouth to urge her to answer, even though I assumed she'd turn me down...
``...I'm sorry, Hanyuu.``
``I need a little... yes, just a little time.@k@vS16/47/DS43010487. Would you give me some time to think...?``

``I believe what you said is right on the mark.@k@vS16/47/DS43010489. However, I still don't have the courage needed to make that choice.@k@vS16/47/DS43010490. ...So would you give me just a little more time to sort through how I feel?``
This was a shock. I never thought she'd agree to leave the village.
It was for that reason... I could see the depths of Ouka's concerns in the pained expression on her face.
Even if she abandoned the village... she was still thinking about how to resolve the current issue.
Was she truly that cornered...?

``...@k@vS16/45/DS43071847. Understood.@k@vS16/45/DS43071848. Then I shall go and make enough medicine to match the current demand.@k@vS16/45/DS43071849. It will be done by tomorrow afternoon, so wait at the shrine, all right?``
``...Yes.@k@vS16/47/DS43010492. Thank you. Goodbye...``
Ouka said that and left the ritual storehouse with a smile. I proceeded to head for Ryuuou in Onigafuchi to make the medicine.
...I exited the swamp and picked up a trunk sitting on the banks.
After looking inside to confirm it was full of the completed medicine, I closed the lid and lifted the trunk with both hands.
``And up we... go...``
It was fairly heavy, but not enough that I couldn't carry it.
Since I couldn't shift into my astral form while carrying a physical object, I took a deserted animal trail back to Furude Shrine.
``Still...``
...I had a bad feeling.
I'd actually happened to come across Shino and Mao yesterday on my way to the swamp.
Mao was accompanied by several young subordinates and had called for a palanquin as if she were planning to go far away.
Then she walked away from the others and met Shino in front of the gate. They seemed to be talking about something.
(What could it be?)
It was odd to see this pairing even though I knew they'd made up, so I quietly approached them in my astral form.
And when I did...
``...All right, Mao-san.@k@vS16/00/DS43030395. Please take care of it.``
``Yeah.@k@vS16/00/DS43040194. I'll try bowing to my uncle and persuading him to wait a while before talking to the feudal lord about the medicine.``
``We could accept it if it were just about the monopoly or the prescription.@k@vS16/00/DS43030397. But there is no way we can consent to the other matter.``
(Other matter...?)
Their faces were oddly serious and resolute... so my heart leapt when I saw that.
What were they talking about...?
Was there more to this story than what I'd heard from Ouka?

``Still, it sure as hell isn't funny.@k@vS16/00/DS43040197. The feudal lord's got quite some balls to take advantage of another country suffering from the plague by using the demon medicine as a bargaining chip.@k@vS16/00/DS43040198. Ouka would faint if she heard about it.``

``You're right.@k@vS16/00/DS43030399. ...Saving people is one thing, but she would definitely never allow it to be used as a threat to boss people around.@k@vS16/00/DS43030400. To think he'd use it as a tool of war... How revolting.``
I stood still in shock, and desperately fought down the fury that threatened to erupt from me.
...According to Shino and Mao, the feudal lord was thinking of using the provision of the medicine as a strategic tool against a neighboring country he was fighting with over territory.
That country was having just as much trouble with this disease as this one. So if it could be used as an effective solution for that, politicians would jump right at it.
But to work their own inhuman greed into that...
How terrible... How could this feudal lord conceive such a cruel and vile plan?!
``Not sure if we'll be able to convince him, though...@k@vS16/00/DS43040200. Anyway, what about you, Shino-san?``

``I was going to negotiate with the next village over, in preparation should worst come to worst.@k@vS16/00/DS43030402. We left some reserve medicine in their warehouse should the plague resurface, so worst-case scenario we'll try to store it there.``
``I don't want Ouka to hear about any of this, at the very least.@k@vS16/00/DS43030404. Let's do what we can to take care of this by ourselves.``
``Yeah, I'll be counting on you... Later.``
The two nodded at each other, and Mao got in the palanquin while Shino resumed her stroll.
As I watched that... my belief that Ouka shouldn't stay in this village grew stronger and stronger.
(I'll try persuading her again.)
She didn't belong in such an ugly and filthy world.
I couldn't hesitate anymore, even if it meant getting a little pushy. For I was sure that she'd be happier if I took her out of the village...
I kept parting through thickets and bushes as I made my way down the mountain, completely forgetting to even rest.
I unknowingly picked up the pace. My anxiety kept welling up when I was alone, and I could no longer bear it.
...Funny. It didn't feel like it'd been that long since I left the swamp. So why?
Well, whatever. I was sure this gloom would all go away once I saw Ouka's face.
Ouka would surely be thrilled once I brought her the medicine. And if I told her what Shino and Mao had discussed yesterday, it'd likely strengthen her resolve to leave the village.
Then I'd feel better. I'd surely be saved.
So...
So please...
But...
When I got to the shrine where Ouka lived...
I saw villagers with wide bloodshot eyes, each carrying their own weapons.
They were surrounding someone, their hands bound with straw rope.
Dangling from the highest tree in the area... was Ouka, covered in wounds.
I was so stunned that I completely forgot that I'd dropped the trunk.
...What was going on?
What in the world happened...?
``Hahaha...``
``Heheheh...``
The villagers gave vulgar smiles as they looked up at Ouka with interest, as though she were one of the bandits who'd been put on display the other day.
Many of them were those who'd barely escaped death thanks to my medicine... no, nearly all of them were!
Ouka was hanging from a tree.
Her pure white miko robes were stained redder and blacker than her skirt.
The sleeves I'd teased for being a little too big for her had been torn cruelly, perhaps remnants of some severe punishment, exposing her white skin.
Her dark hair, which I'd praised many a time for looking beautiful when the sun shined on it, had lost its luster and was disheveled.
Her cute face, white as porcelain, was black and blue all over, a shadow of its former self.
When I looked to see how the villagers saw her... I felt violent shivers run down my spine.
Their eyes held the bloodlust of ferocious carnivores, and were glazed over with disgusting curiosity and avarice.
These were... these were the villagers Ouka had so earnestly tried to help...?!
``How about it, miko~? Feel like answering?``
Ouka just groaned. That was all the more painful to look at.
``Hey, did you hear me?``
Even so, the man next to her paid that no heed, and lifted her chin with the timber he held.
``Hmph... The cold shoulder, huh? I admit, you're more tenacious than I thought.``
``...You sure about this? Don't you think beating her so badly will only make her less inclined to talk?``
``No worries. We may have killed her husband, but her daughter still lives. If we bring her out when she's weak, she won't be able to keep quiet.``
``Alive, huh...? I suppose so, in the sense that she's still breathing...``
What?!
``Relax, we didn't kill her or anything. Though we had a little scuffle in the living room until she started coughing up blood and keeled over on her own.``
``Anyway, your daughter's safe. But she just wouldn't stop crying, so we kicked her around until she stopped moving, though~.``
Ouka's eyes widened in shock as she shivered, forgetting to even sob.
Those inhuman scum...!! How dare they... How dare they...!!
``Hey... is it really okay to do this? She is still Oyashiro-sama's miko after all...``
``It's fine, these are the people who sold their souls to a demon! If anything, that makes us demon slaying heroes, doesn't it? Ahahahaha!!``
Demon slaying...? They call this demon slaying...?!
I see... these people are that sect that came to Onigafuchi recently!
I was concerned with the odd way they were looking at Ouka and the others when I saw them the other day... but they don't know the terror of ``demons``...!
That was... That was why they attacked the Furude family, a supposedly taboo act that would invoke divine wrath... and did this to Ouka...!!
``Anyway... is there really a treasure? The feudal lord's already prepared his forces, so our lives will be on the line if we come back empty-handed.``
``Yeah, it's all fine! I followed the miko first to make sure! It seems there's a secret in Oyashiro-sama's well... Onigafuchi.``
``...Well, even if there isn't, we still have the medicine from last time, so we can make another killing selling that!``
...Huh?

``But boy oh boy, is this miko too nice for her own good. All we had to do was fake a little illness for her to give us the medicine for free. I never thought we'd get so rich selling it off at a high price to the ill in the next village over!``
What... did they... just say...?
``Well, we'll have to make her work for the ones that were duds. Because we thought we'd benefit from her if we let her play leader and flattered her.``
Playing leader... and... flattered her?!
``I guess it's an admirable mindset and all to keep telling everyone to get along like an idiot. Not that we give a damn what happens to those people on the other side of the mountain!``
``Yeah, that's right! They're not like us! And besides, we...``
``Have a demon on our side.``
...Who?
Who did you say... was on your side?
For these lowlifes...
For these ignorant, cowardly, vulgar, shameless, arrogant, lying, savage, wretched scum, I...!
Me... and... Ouka... were...!
OUKA WAAAAAAAAAAAAS!!!
``...? Hey, what's wrong?``
He suddenly stopped moving and opened his mouth on the spot.
And the moment his shoulder was tapped...
Roll...
Something like a stone mass fell to the ground with an oddly dull sound and rolled at his feet.
The other man looked down to see what it was... and found a human face.
And then...
``E-eeeeeeeeeeek?!``
Dark red blood spurted out like a small geyser and poured down on the faces and bodies of the men around.
Standing on the other side of the headless body...
Was...
None other than...
``Eee, eeeek... hyaaaaaaaaaaaaah!``
``A-a demon...! There's a demon heeeeeeeere!!!``
Grrrrgh... Grrrrrrrgh...!!
``...You bastards...!``
``Aah... aaaaaaaaah...!!``
``S-save... save us...!!``
``You bastards... harmed Ouka... My Oukaaaaaaa!!``
``Gyaaaaaaaaagh!!``
``Ghh... ghhghaaaagh...?!``
``Eeeeeee... Eeeeeeeeeeek...!!``
``I deny your right to live!!``
``I revoke your privilege to live!``
``Die... You humans...!!``
When I came to, my surroundings were engulfed in flames.
Leaves burned and scattered into black ashes that danced in violent gales of blazing heat.
Flowers turned to charcoal, and where thick foliage once stood was now wreathed in crimson hellfire from the lowest depths of Hell.
And as for the earth...
It was mantled with countless rivers of blood and a sea of unidentifiable corpses, leaving the ground only visible through what faint gaps lay between.
I swung my hand, shaking off the blood splatter that'd manicured my extended claws.
And then I turned around to face the three brave... no, foolish warriors pointing swords at me, and approached them one step after another to the chorus of my feet rubbing against the gravel.
``S... stay away... stay awaaaaay...!!``
The men in front of me slowly backed away in rhythm with my own steps, their swords and bodies shaking.
They probably assumed I'd chase them and cut them down if they turned their backs to me.
It was quite admirable. They were shedding large drops of sweat clearly not induced by the immense heat, yet still focused their fearful gaze on me.
It surely would have been effective if I were a bear or beast... But I was no beast, and like the ``god`` they spoke of every day, I held little mercy.
I closed the distance between us in the blink of an eye, thrust my claws at the three soldiers, and skewered each and every one of them without hesitation.
One in the throat.
And the one opposite of him in the heart.

The final one, a man who had taken a mere two steps back further than his companions, instinctively tried to turn around, but I seized that moment to pierce his medulla oblongata down to the base of my fingers.
The three fell to the ground and stopped moving without another word.
My hands were already sticky with blood, dulling the sharpness of my claws when I mowed them down.
Still, these slender claws were sharper than any weapon they possessed, meaning I could easily penetrate their fragile skin virtually effortlessly.
(...How many people are there left?)
I tried to remember how much of my ``prey`` remained as I caught my breath.
...But I soon realized it was futile and gave it up.
In the several hours since this began, I could remember I killed at least five commanding officers, the leader of which I had defeated with little effort just a few minutes ago.
It was only natural to scramble to flee after losing your commander, but the majority of the forces they'd sent were currently in retreat, leaving only a few stragglers in this area.
There was no need to seek further prey now that the enemy had lost their will to fight, and once their numbers piled up again, their doom was sealed.
Suddenly, a large tree near me started creaking from the roots and leaning before eventually falling to the ground with a loud crash.
I gave it a blank look of no interest, then made my way through the thicket and arrived at high ground that served as a good vantage point.
The moonlit village was speckled with the flickering of torchlights.
Just a short while ago, they had displayed unbroken unity and coordination, as well as vast numbers, until they came here...
But now there were less than half of the torches I saw earlier, grouped up awkwardly here and there with no conformity.
...Pitiful.
I looked down upon the pandemonium scornfully, my heart cold enough to give even me shivers.
They brought this on themselves with their curiosity.
God smote them with divine punishment for their overabundant greed and pride.
Indeed. Right now, at this very instant, I was their god.
``Ahahahaha...@k@|@y@vS16/45/DS43071878. Ahahahahahahahaha...!!``
``S-save meeeee!!``
``A-a demon... There's a demon hereeeee!!``
Gwoooooogh, gwoooooooogh...!!
Fire.
Everything was burning, swallowed by flames that could reach the heavens.
I witnessed the crimson lotuses from hell blossoming, and those who desperately fled from its menace.
I heard screams and sobs, as well as the roar of homes and storehouses collapsing to the flames.
The creatures of this world particularly feared fire. ...That was proof their cultural and intelligence level was very immature.
Since I'd gone down to the settlement, I had expected someone brave or foolish wielding a sword or something to face me.
...However, it seemed they were prioritizing their own protection above all else, and thus were in no state of mind to be doing otherwise.
Suddenly, my eyes turned toward the shadow of a building.
A mother, most likely late in evacuating, was carrying her small daughter and shivering as she looked at me.
Her eyes swayed with fear and... hatred?
But, was it because they were weak that they failed to understand that hatred alone was not enough to take on a foe?
Yes. The weak should know their place.

They should reflect long and hard about their own foolishness, in allowing themselves to ruthlessly hurt their fellow man out of desire for things beyond their reach, and whether that can be forgiven.
In hell... the world after death you all speak of.
``Kihyahahahahahaha!!@k@|@y@vS16/45/DS43071881. Ahyahahahahahahahaha!!!``
Having mostly cleared away the stragglers, I leisurely walked straight down a path.
``Heehee, eheeheeheeheeheeheeheehee!@k@vS16/45/DS43071883. Ahahahahahahahahaha...!``
...Aah, I could hear something.
Voices were approaching.
The noisy sounds were gradually replaced by human voices.
Their words were all likely those of grief, fear, desperation... and despair.

I didn't need to bother straining my ears to hear them anymore. It was only natural they'd be like that when their only escape route, the bridge connecting Onigafuchi Village to the adjacent village, had collapsed.
The more they thought they were saved, the greater their despair when they were driven to death. I wondered what kind of faces they were making when faced with the deep ravine.
...When I thought that, something like rapture seethed in my heart, and I smiled bitterly.
(...So in the end, I'm a demon... huh?)

I'd thought I'd abandoned all my emotions, yet my blood raged in desire of prey. No matter how much I tried to smooth things over, no one would believe me when they saw my claws and fangs suited for battle.
...Not even Ouka, surely.
I felt a little lonely when I thought that.
Even so...
This was the only path I had left.
That's right... I'm sure she'll... I'm sure Ouka will understand.
She should understand...
I suddenly stood up and strained my ears.
...The screams had gotten a lot quieter.
I could hear miserable sobs in their place.
Have they finally given up? ...No, they're probably still offering prayers that will never be answered, desperately wanting to cling to something.
So be it. I'll hear you out.
I am Oyashiro-sama. The supreme god you ignorant, cowardly, insignificant lowlifes worship.
Now, pray. And repent.
For your conceited actions. Your foolish thoughts.
Do so, and I will at least begin granting death to those who fervently pray for as little pain as possible.
Though there shall be no respite from your pain. Heheheh...!
``Ahahahaha...@k@vS16/45/DS43071878. Ahahahahahahaha...!!``
Now then, the festival is almost over.
What death shall I grant to those huddled and trembling in fear?
Just as I thought that, and smirked while licking my lips...
A human presence stopped behind me.
I sensed bloodlust... No, that's not what this pure sensation was.
Assuming it was one of the soldiers from the hunt, I turned around.
I had only halfway turned my body.
I was frozen. I couldn't move.
There was no mistaking it. It was Ouka standing there.
Backed by the flames of the burning village, wearing her tattered and filthy miko robes.
In her right hand... she wielded a dully shining sword.
``Ouka...@k@vS16/45/DS43071890. A-are your wounds all right...?``
Not having expected her to have the strength to make it this far, the first thing I did was inquire about her wellbeing.
However...
Ouka silently stared straight at me... with a cold expression.
Before long, Ouka's face warped in agony, perhaps from the pain of her wounds, and she fell to her knees. I ran over to her in a hurry as soon as I saw that.
``A-are you all right?@k@vS16/45/DS43071893. Ou...?!``
It happened in an instant.
I didn't understand what had just occurred.
No, that wasn't it.
My brain was rejecting the reality before me.
Ouka... swung her blade at me.
Blood poured from the cut on my cheek made from the sword's tip.
It wasn't a major wound, and it didn't hurt. And yet... I felt a cold impact... hit my head at full force.
``Wh-what are you...?``
``...Your weak point...``
Ouka muttered that as she placed the blade of the sword on her palm, closed her hand, and bathed the tip in her blood. Then, the moment she lifted her head, she swiftly closed the distance and swung!
``Waaaaaa?!``
I immediately readied my claws and blocked the blow.
But my claws which should have easily intercepted the sword broke off like hard candy at the base when they collided, flew into the air, and pierced the ground.
``The same kind of blood is a double-edged sword.@k@vS16/47/DS43010502. It prevents your body from hardening... right?``
``One more thing.@k@vS16/47/DS43010504. The same goes for the barrier... protecting your body!!``
``Gwaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!!``
I was cleaved diagonally and staggered as I held my unprotected right shoulder.
Lukewarm yet vivid blood gushed out of my wound, and intense pain that threatened to render me unconscious rushed through my whole body.
That was right... I remembered.
Over 20 years ago... when I suffered an injury outside of a battle with a Grifys.
Right after I fought that bear at the swamp and injured my foot on a tree root thanks to a careless step.
And the one who helped me back then... was the father of the kind person in front of me.
And his daughter was wielding a sword, and injured me...?!
``Wh-why... Ouka?!``
Ouka didn't answer. She just silently stained her sword in blood and closed the distance, as if she spotted an opening.
Her brow looked slightly furrowed.
I wanted to believe... it was her pain surfacing.
``Ouka, why?@k@vS16/45/DS43071904. Why are you swinging that sword at me?!``
``It doesn't make sense, does it? It's strange, right?@k@vS16/45/DS43071906. I loved you!@k@vS16/45/DS43071907. I listened to your every wish!@k@vS16/45/DS43071908. Even the unreasonable ones!``
``I said I wanted to help you when I learned you were in danger!@k@vS16/45/DS43071910. I meant that!@k@vS16/45/DS43071911. So... So why?!``
Ouka quietly readied the sword in both hands, blood still pouring from her palms.
I could tell from the aura in her dark eyes she intended to defeat me with her next strike.
``Humans... can not live... with demons...``
Even so...

``I had a dream...@k@vS16/47/DS43010510. A dream where I had fun with you and my family.@k@vS16/47/DS43010511. I believed my daughter would love you for sure...@k@vS16/47/DS43010512. Because I loved you too...``
Her eyes were faintly... no, clearly blurry with tears.
I didn't understand.
Why was she looking at me with such sorrow?
``I have the most fun when I'm with you.``
You said that... You said that to me with a smile, Ouka... so why?!!
``Why are you protecting those lowlifes?@k@vS16/45/DS43071914. They tricked you!@k@vS16/45/DS43071915. Not only that, they tried to use you and take advantage of you!``
``They don't deserve to live after that!@k@vS16/45/DS43071917. And they ravaged you and your family!!``

``They deserved to die!@k@vS16/45/DS43071919. They trampled on our feelings and the time we spent together!@k@vS16/45/DS43071920. Nothing benefits from their proliferation! They are the lowest form of creature there is!!@k@vS16/45/DS43071921. No, it is revolting to even consider them in the same category as creatures!``
``They are trash who leave havoc in their wake and devour everything this world has to offer like gluttons!@k@vS16/45/DS43071923. Isn't that right, Ouka?!``
Large teardrops... spilled forth from Ouka's eyes.
``I, too... am one of those lowest forms of creatures.@k@vS16/47/DS43010517. Am I trash, Mother...?``
She knew...? She dealt with me knowing about the circumstances of her birth from the very start...?!
``...Y-you're wrong!@k@vS16/45/DS43071926. I'm not your...!``
``I'm not wrong!``
Eventually...
Ouka slowly... ever so slowly... looked up, and smiled.
``And there is no question I brought forth this tragedy.@k@vS16/47/DS43010520. Because I made an unreasonable request of you, Mother.@k@vS16/47/DS43010521. ...Because I depended on you...!``
``T-that's not...``
I wrung out my shaking voice.
I wanted to deny it.
The happy time I'd gained in this world... Meeting Riku, and giving birth to Ouka.
I couldn't accept that they were linked to this tragedy. By no means could I accept it!
``So... I am the one who should be judged.@k@vS16/47/DS43010523. And to sacrifice one's life in order to protect this village from disaster... is the fate of those born of the Furude family...!``
``Why... Why does it have to be this way, Ouka?!``
You're wrong.
You're wrong, Ouka!
A fate like that doesn't suit you!
Ouka deserved the right to live a far happier life!
changeReason: New names 'Ouka' explicitly where old uses 'You', and adds 'far happier' vs just 'happier', changing specificity and emotional intensity.

TextENGNew: Ouka raised the sword to a middle guard. And then--
changeReason: Old says 'thrust the tip of the sword out'; new says 'raised the sword to a middle guard' (正眼に構える = seigan stance), correcting the described action.

TextENGNew: ``Come at me!@k@vS16/47/DS43010526. You -- MONSTEEEEEER!!!``
``U... UWAAAAAAAAAAAA--!!!``
